content.problemStatement:
Hrishi's daughters likes to eat laddoos a lot. 


There are **N** sets of laddoos prepared for Diwali; for each **i** (1 ≤ **i** ≤ **N**), the **i**-th set contains **Ai** laddoos.



Hrishi will come home in **H** hours and his daughters want to finish all the laddoos before he returns.



His daughters can eat **K** laddoos per hour. 
Each hour, they will choose some set of laddoos. 
- If this set contains at least **K** laddoos, then they will eat exactly **K** ladoos from it. 
- Otherwise, they eat the whole set but they wont eat from any other set during this hour.

What is the **minimum** **K** such that they are able to eat all the laddoos in **H** hours.


Input Format
The first line of the input contains a single integer **T** denoting the number of test cases. The description of **T** test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers **N** and **H** denoting the number of sets and the number of hours after which Hrishi will come home.
The second line contains **N** space-separated integers **A1, A2, ..., AN**.
Output
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum possible value of **K**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two space‑separated integers N and H. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, ..., AN representing the number of laddoos in each set.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum possible integer K (laddoos per hour) that allows all laddoos to be eaten within H hours.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case we binary‑search the smallest K such that sum_i ceil(Ai/K) ≤ H. The computed minima are 3, 2 and 4 respectively, matching the explanation in the statement.
With a single set of 100 laddoos and 10 hours, K must be at least ceil(100/10)=10. K=10 works because it needs exactly 10 hours.
Each set contains only one laddoo, so K=1 finishes each set in one hour, using exactly 5 hours.

solution.approach: For a fixed speed K, the number of hours needed to finish a set with Ai laddoos is ceil(Ai / K) because each hour the daughters work on a single set, eating K laddoos if the set still has at least K, otherwise they finish the set. The total hours required for a given K is sum_i ceil(Ai / K). The condition sum_i ceil(Ai / K) ≤ H must hold. The function f(K) = sum_i ceil(Ai / K) is monotonic non‑increasing with K, therefore we can binary‑search the smallest K in the range [1, max(Ai)]. During the check we accumulate the hours and stop early if the sum exceeds H to keep the check O(N). The overall complexity per test case is O(N log maxAi) with O(1) extra space besides the input array.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log M) per test case, where M = max(Ai) (≤ 10^9).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the array of laddoo counts.
